idea: Platform where gamers can hire another players and pay for sessions which they play together. Tip if they help really well

competitors.name: E-Pal
competitors.description: A two-sided marketplace where gamers hire vetted ‘ePals’ for in-game companionship, coaching, and social/lifestyle interactions.

details.targetMarket: Casual and competitive gamers looking for teammates, companionship, coaching, or streamers/creators seeking monetization and audience growth

items: Browse ePal profiles by game, service type and rating
Place orders for sessions (gameplay, coaching, social/lifestyle services)
In-app chat and voice for sessions
Live section and Arcade mini-games to discover active ePals
Buff virtual currency (1 Buff = $1) for purchases and tips
E-Coin for in-chat gifts and team-room gifting
Withdrawals to PayPal for ePals (withdraw rules and hold periods applied)
Tips, reviews and creator/coach badges to surface sellers
Twitch integration (sync streams and panels)
Creator and Coach badges (application process for qualified sellers)
Streamer Growth Project and leaderboards to promote top creators
Review system and refund flow (3-day refund window for completed orders)
Verification options for creators/streamers
Community guidelines, multimedia and platform policies
Web platform and mobile app presence (download links on site)

missingFeatures: Limited payment options (PayPal-only for recharges/withdrawals; no card/Stripe/alternate local payments exposed)
No clearly documented standard pricing or packaged coaching subscriptions — prices are set per-ePal and can be inconsistent
Limited built-in scheduling / calendar / recurring booking workflow for regular coaching clients
Discovery and matchmaking appear primarily profile-driven (opportunity for stronger algorithmic matching)
Few B2B / publisher integrations or exposed APIs for teams, game publishers or enterprise partners

pricingPlans.name: ePal orders (per-session)
pricingPlans.note: ePals set individual prices per order/session. Payments use Buff (1 Buff = $1).

pricingPlans.name: VIP Plus (subscription)
pricingPlans.note: Monthly/seasonal/annual tiers. Includes free Buffs on subscribe, unlimited messaging, extra Meet features and other platform perks.

pricingPlans.name: VIP Premium (subscription)
pricingPlans.note: Higher-tier subscription with larger free Buff allowance, exclusive effects and additional perks. Exact prices are handled through platform stores (PayPal / Apple / Google).

strengths: Large two-sided user base and network effects for matching gamers and ePals (millions of users reported)
Rich set of social features: voice chat, mini-games (Arcade), team rooms and Twitch integration
Clear monetization for creators via orders, tips, virtual currencies and subscriptions
weaknesses: Narrow payment support (PayPal-only) increases friction for some buyers and sellers
Fragmented pricing — per-ePal custom rates make price discovery and standardized offerings harder
Limited professional tooling for coaches (scheduling, invoicing, recurring sessions) and few enterprise integrations

competitors.name: Metafy
competitors.description: A creator-first marketplace to book live gaming coaching, buy on-demand guides, join community subscriptions and attend group events — targeting gamers and creators who monetize game expertise.

details.targetMarket: Amateur and semi-pro gamers, streamers, and creators seeking paid coaching and creators/coaches who want to monetize skills

items: Book 1:1 live coaching sessions (set price & availability)
Buy on-demand Guides (video or written)
Events (group classes, bootcamps, watch parties)
Creator profile pages with reviews and featured content
Metafy Core revenue-share model (8% fee) and Metafy Pro (fixed monthly fee, keep 100%)
Subscription-based Communities / Supporter tiers
Training Plans and bundled session packages
Tiers, perks and Discord integration for gated community access
Scheduling/booking and messaging between student and coach
Search and discovery by game/skill/coach reputation
Payouts and withdrawal tools for creators
Basic analytics and earnings estimation tools

missingFeatures: Native in-browser/live lesson tooling (video, recording, whiteboard) — many creators rely on Discord or external tools
Structured learner progress tracking/certifications or curriculum with measurable outcomes
Robust enterprise / team offerings for schools, esports orgs, or educational partners
Deeper learner analytics and coach performance dashboards for continuous improvement
Flexible student subscription/bundle payment options (multi-session subscriptions, family plans)
Advanced AI-assisted coach matching, personalized learning paths, or automated curriculum generation

pricingPlans.name: Core
pricingPlans.note: No monthly fee; platform takes ~8% revenue share on earnings (Core is recommended for new creators).

pricingPlans.name: Pro (monthly)
pricingPlans.note: Fixed monthly fee; creators keep 100% of earnings. Monthly billing shown at $29/mo.

pricingPlans.name: Pro (yearly)
pricingPlans.note: Annual billing option (advertised as save 33%) — effectively $19/mo billed yearly.

strengths: Creator-first monetization (clear Core vs Pro choices and revenue-share model)
Multiple product channels: 1:1 sessions, Guides, Events, and subscription Communities
Brand recognition in gaming and a growing network of high-profile coaches
Rapid feature iteration and visible product roadmap (active help center & updates)
weaknesses: Many lessons currently rely on external tools (Discord) rather than a fully native lesson experience
Limited structured learning paths and progress tracking for students
Marketplace discoverability can be noisy for new creators without promotion
Mobile and in-platform native live features have historically lagged behind expectations

competitors.name: ProGuides
competitors.description: Paid gaming coaching and training platform offering on-demand courses, 1:1 coaching and gameplay analysis for competitive gamers.

details.funding: $5M seed (reported; later acquired by Statespace in 2021)
details.targetMarket: Competitive and aspiring esports players, casual players who want to improve, and fans who want to learn from pro gamers.

items: Video courses from pro players
Structured lessons and bootcamps
Playstyle and role-specific guides
1:1 live coaching sessions (bookable on-demand)
Play-with-the-pro sessions / scrims
Coach discovery and ratings
Upload gameplay for coach analysis (Gameplay Analysis)
VALORANT StatsCenter and match metrics
Run It Back: personalized Aimlabs tasks from deaths
Aimlabs+ membership integration
Discovery AI to generate training tasks and recommendations
Personalized aim training tasks and analytics

missingFeatures: Transparent, genuinely monthly billing option (users report annual-only charge despite advertised monthly price)
Robust team/org features for pro teams or coaches managing rosters (B2B product)
Verified, standardized coach certification or quality-assurance program
Deeper long-term learning paths, progress certifications or accredited curricula
Public API or integrations for third-party platforms (analytics export, LMS integration)
Localized content and broader language support

pricingPlans.name: Pro Member
pricingPlans.note: Entry-level membership granting access to courses and member benefits (commonly marketed at $7.99/mo but often billed annually).

pricingPlans.name: Pay-per-session (PG Points)
pricingPlans.note: Coaching and gameplay analysis are purchased per session using PG Points; price depends on coach and session length.

pricingPlans.name: Aimlabs+ / Combined Membership
pricingPlans.note: Aimlabs+ and ProGuides memberships have integrated benefits (discounts on coaching, Aimlabs tasks and Discovery AI).

strengths: Large library of pro-led courses and lessons across multiple competitive titles
Marketplace model with thousands of bookable coaches and on-demand sessions
Tight integration with Aimlabs tools (Discovery AI, personalized tasks) and proprietary match analytics
Affordable entry price point compared with private coaching, and discounts for members
weaknesses: Pricing transparency and billing complaints from users (annual charges despite advertised monthly rate)
Quality variability between coaches—limited standardized certification
Primarily B2C focus; limited enterprise/team features for esports organizations
Dependent on third-party freelance coaches for capacity and quality control

competitors.name: Gamer Sensei
competitors.description: A marketplace connecting players with professional esports coaches for live 1:1 and team coaching sessions to improve competitive gameplay.

details.business: Marketplace / B2C coaching (also offers B2B team coaching)
details.targetMarket: Individual competitive gamers (casual to aspiring pro), esports teams, parents purchasing coaching for children

items: Coach directory and searchable profiles
Coach ratings and reviews
Coach-set pricing (hourly/lesson rates)
Scheduling and booking system
One-on-one live lessons
Team coaching and group sessions
Custom lesson plans and curriculum
Skills assessments (flat-rate assessment offering announced)
Built-in video conferencing (added in 2021)
In-platform messaging and scheduling sync
Lesson packages and unscheduled-hours credit/refund system
Coach onboarding and career development support
In-platform payments and credit system
Refunds/credits for canceled lessons
Coach-managed pricing tiers (novice to pro/legendary)

missingFeatures: Subscription or membership plans for recurring coaching (mostly pay-per-lesson)
Robust progress analytics / learning dashboard to track player improvement over time
Stronger, standardized coach certification and transparent vetting details
Native in-game integrations or overlays for live coach spectating and replay annotation
Mobile-first app experience and easier localized language support for non-English markets

pricingPlans.name: Pay-per-lesson
pricingPlans.note: Coach-set hourly rates; typical range reported around $16–$100+ per hour depending on coach skill level.

pricingPlans.name: Team Coaching
pricingPlans.note: Custom pricing for team sessions and packages (offers team coaching services).

strengths: Early mover and recognizable brand in esports coaching with a large coach network
Wide game coverage and coach variety (roles, skill levels, former pros)
Flexible, coach-driven pricing enabling entry-level and premium options
Acquired by Corsair (2020) — access to resources and broader esports partnerships
weaknesses: Inconsistent coach quality and uneven user experience due to marketplace model
Lack of standardized progress-tracking or learning analytics for students
No clear subscription/retention product — relies on one-off lesson purchases
Pricing can be expensive for casual players; limited low-cost learning paths

competitors.name: Eloking
competitors.description: A boosting, coaching and duo-queue teammate service for competitive gamers seeking faster rank progression in titles like League of Legends, Valorant and CS:GO.

details.targetMarket: Individual competitive gamers who want rank boosts, coaching, or to play duo/teammate sessions in games such as League of Legends, Valorant, CS:GO, Dota 2, Overwatch 2 and Rocket League.

items: Rank/division boosting (division-by-division)
Placement and win-based boosts
Duo / teammate sessions (play-with a pro)
Coaching sessions with vetted pros
Faceit / platform-specific boosts (CS)
Online boosting price calculator
24/7 live support / order support
Screened professional boosters (claim top players/pros)
Refunds and a loyalty program (cashback, discounts, daily lootbox)
Multi-game support across major competitive titles
Regional pricing adjustments
Encrypted payments (Stripe integration)
Order tracking and member area

missingFeatures: Live, verifiable in-game streaming or replay evidence per order (better proof of booster activity)
Subscription / recurring coaching plans for long-term skill development
Official API or in-product game-account verification to reduce account-sharing risk
Dedicated mobile app for ordering and tracking
Transparent booster performance metrics (win-rate, average completion time) on each booster profile

pricingPlans.name: Pay-per-order
pricingPlans.note: Primary pricing model: calculator computes cost per order (per-division / per-win). Example low-rank rate cited ~€6 per division in some articles/region pages.

pricingPlans.name: Coaching / Teammate
pricingPlans.note: One-off coaching or duo sessions priced per session; rates depend on coach rank and game.

strengths: Competitive, calculator-driven pricing with regional adjustments
Wide game support and multiple service types (boosts, coaching, teammate)
Strong social proof (Trustpilot reviews, site testimonials) and 24/7 support
Vetted boosters and a loyalty program to encourage repeat customers
weaknesses: Boosting is inherently against many games' terms of service — reputational and account-risk exposure
Limited public company transparency (funding, team size and growth not clearly published)
No clear standardized subscription or long-term coaching packages for retention
Lack of verifiable per-booster performance metrics and live proof for orders

competitors.name: LegionFarm
competitors.description: A marketplace connecting everyday gamers with pro players for coaching, duo-queue companionship, and boosting services.

details.targetMarket: Casual and competitive gamers seeking one-on-one coaching, duo-queue play, account boosting, and companionship across AAA multiplayer titles

items: One-on-one coaching sessions with vetted pro players
Duo-queue / play-with-a-pro (companion) sessions
Carry / boosting services to achieve in-game goals
Group play sessions (multi-customer sessions with a Pro)
Session scheduling and calendar
VOD / replay review and coach feedback
Subscription option for recurring access to group sessions
Per-hour and package pricing for flexible buys
Onboarding and tiers for Pros (Semi-Pro/Pro roles)
Tools for Pros to accept orders and manage sessions
Promotions, incentives and NFT/guild-style offerings (experimental)

missingFeatures: Clear, standardized coach quality metrics and verified credentials (hard to compare coaches objectively)
Structured, progressive curricula and learning paths tied to measurable outcomes
Robust in-app progress analytics and long-term skill tracking for customers
Enterprise / team-focused offerings for esports orgs or schools
Transparent packaged pricing bundles (most offerings are pay-per-session or subscription without consistent bundled learning plans)

pricingPlans.name: Subscription (Standard)
pricingPlans.note: Monthly subscription that grants access to group play sessions and discounts; company previously describes $50/mo as the default subscription tier.

pricingPlans.name: Pay-per-session
pricingPlans.note: Individual 1:1 or duo sessions charged hourly; historically examples reported ~$12/hr for some services but rates vary by game and pro level.

pricingPlans.name: Top-up / Senior
pricingPlans.note: Higher-value top-ups (e.g. $500) that have been promoted with added benefits/subscription access.

strengths: Large network of professional players across multiple AAA titles
Multiple monetization models (subscription + pay-per-session + packages)
Product features focused on live coaching, VOD review and scheduling
YC exposure / notable investor backing and solid early traction
weaknesses: Inconsistent transparency around coach qualification and performance metrics
Limited structured learning pathways and long-term progress tracking
Pricing and packages can feel fragmented for users who want clear outcomes
Primary focus on consumer sessions — limited B2B/enterprise products

competitors.name: Boosting Factory
competitors.description: Competitive-gaming services provider offering rank boosting, coaching, duo-queue and account services for players across multiple titles and platforms.

details.targetMarket: Competitive and casual players who want fast rank progression, account leveling, coaching or play-with/duo services across PC and console.

items: Rank/division boosting (per-division and full placement boosts)
Duo-queue / Play-with booster (play on customer's account alongside booster)
Coaching sessions and one-on-one coaching
Account leveling, wins boosting and placement matches
Platform & region support across major titles (LoL, Valorant, CoD, OW, Apex, CS2, etc.)
Real-time order tracking and booster chat
Scheduling / pause/resume boost orders
Live streaming option (extra fee)
Loyalty program with tiered discounts and cashback
VPN IP-mimicking for booster sessions and anonymous accounts
Express orders and priority starts
Custom requests (specific heroes/agents/champions)
Money-back guarantee and dispute support
Multiple payment methods (PayPal commonly supported)

missingFeatures: Transparent, public pricing matrix/searchable price calculator without entering an order
Public, verifiable booster profiles (match history, verified stats and individual ratings)
Ongoing subscription/coaching plans for recurring skill development
Mobile app or first-class push/notification experience for order updates
Clearer, standardized SLAs or insurance around bans/penalties (buyer protection)

pricingPlans.name: Per-order (Boosting / Duo / Play-with)
pricingPlans.note: Pay-per-service. Prices vary by game, start/end rank, platform, extras (streaming, express). Loyalty discounts available.

pricingPlans.name: Coaching / Sessions
pricingPlans.note: One-off coaching sessions and coaching packages; price depends on coach and duration.

strengths: Established operator (site and copyright show operation since 2015)
Wide game coverage and multi-platform support
Feature-rich ordering experience: scheduling, pause/resume, chat, live-streaming
Loyalty program that grants permanent discounts and cashback
Generally positive reviewer sentiment (high Trustpilot scores / many positive reviews)
weaknesses: No public, granular pricing visible without configuring an order (friction for buyers)
Limited transparency on individual boosters (few verifiable public profiles)
Potential ToS/legal risk for customers (account sharing/boosting violates many publishers' terms)
Small company footprint / limited corporate disclosure (funding & HQ unclear)
Some customer complaints about booster performance or start delays in reviews

competitors.name: Overgear
competitors.description: A global marketplace connecting gamers with boosters, coaches, and in-game services (duo-play, carries, currency) aimed at MMO and competitive players.

details.funding: Unknown (no public venture funding found; appears bootstrapped)
details.targetMarket: MMO and competitive game players seeking boosting, coaching, in-game currency, carries, and related services

items: Boosting/carry orders (PvE & PvP)
Coaching and lessons (1:1)
In-game currency & item sales
Bundles and weekly curated Vault deals
Play-with/duo services and scheduled runs
Seller vetting / interview process
Escrow-style payment hold until completion
Order tracking and delivery updates
Refunds via Overgear Wallet / OG Coins
Overgear Wallet and OG Coins cashback system
Multiple payment options (cards, Google/Apple Pay, third-party processors)
Promotions, discounts and VIP packages
24/7 live support/chat
Seller dashboards and reputation/ratings
Offer customization and scheduling
Bundles and package creation for sellers

missingFeatures: No site-wide subscription or standardized pricing model (prices vary widely by seller)
Limited public transparency on seller qualifications and consistent quality guarantees beyond basic vetting
No prominent official mobile app experience (web-first; mobile web only) — app could improve convenience and trust
Limited enterprise or team-focused features (bulk/partner tooling, APIs) for studios or large buyers
Potentially slow or confusing KYC/refund flows for some buyers (document requests post-payment reported in forums)

pricingPlans.name: Browse (free)
pricingPlans.note: Create an account, browse offers, and chat with sellers for free; no subscription required.

pricingPlans.name: Pay-as-you-go (marketplace pricing)
pricingPlans.note: Individual service prices are set by sellers (boosts, carries, coaching); Overgear takes a platform fee — final price varies by game, region, and seller.

pricingPlans.name: VIP / Bundles
pricingPlans.note: Curated weekly Vault deals and VIP packages with fixed bundle pricing; prices depend on contents and promotions.

strengths: Large multi-game marketplace with many service categories (boosts, coaching, currency)
Seller-driven pricing keeps costs competitive; platform focuses on low prices and fast delivery
Escrow-like wallet and OG Coins loyalty program improves repeat purchase rates
Seller vetting, order tracking and 24/7 support provide baseline safety for buyers
weaknesses: Quality variability — marketplace model leads to inconsistent service quality between sellers
Regulatory and TOS risk — services (boosting, account-related offers) may conflict with game publishers’ policies
Transparency and trust gaps in some user reports (post-payment KYC requests, delayed deliveries reported on forums)
No clear subscription or enterprise offering for high-volume buyers or partners

competitors.name: Fiverr
competitors.description: A global gig marketplace connecting buyers and freelance sellers across hundreds of service categories (small businesses and individuals buying on-demand digital services, including gaming coaching and play-with-me sessions).

details.targetMarket: Small businesses, startups, marketing teams, and individuals seeking affordable on-demand digital services; freelancers and small agencies selling services

items: Gig-based listings with packages and add-ons
Search, categories, and buyer reviews/ratings
Subscription Gigs (recurring services)
Messaging and order management
Seller dashboards (performance insights, analytics)
Seller programs: Seller Plus, Fiverr Pro, Top Rated Seller badges
Escrow-style order payments and milestone delivery
Multiple payment methods and buyer protection policies
Commission / fee model (platform takes ~20% of seller revenue)
Fiverr Business: team workflows and curated talent
Fiverr Pro: vetted professional freelancers
Fiverr Workspace (formerly AND.CO): invoicing, proposals, contracts
Fiverr Learn courses
AI features integrated across product (search/assistance)
Product features for recurring subscriptions and gig visibility

missingFeatures: Dedicated live/co-play session tooling (scheduling, in-platform low-latency co-play or streaming integration) for gaming services
Stronger standardized vetting and quality guarantees for niche/high-stakes categories (e.g., gaming coaching certifications)
Better matchmaking/recommendation for recurring or long-term engagements (beyond one-off gigs)
More transparent fee-sharing or lower commission options for high-value sellers
Deeper integrations for team collaboration and project management tailored to buyers hiring multiple freelancers

pricingPlans.name: Gig (entry)
pricingPlans.note: Core marketplace model — buyers purchase one-off gigs or packages; sellers set prices (gigs start at $5).

pricingPlans.name: Seller Plus
pricingPlans.note: Subscription for sellers with growth tools, early payouts and promotional features (Standard tier).

pricingPlans.name: Seller Plus Premium
pricingPlans.note: Premium seller subscription with additional business tools and support.

pricingPlans.name: Fiverr Pro Advanced
pricingPlans.note: Advanced subscription for enterprise access to vetted talent and management tools.

pricingPlans.name: Fiverr Workspace
pricingPlans.note: Workspace offers free invoicing and a paid Unlimited plan for freelancers/agencies (pricing varies).

strengths: Large, global marketplace and strong brand awareness
Low-friction buyer experience and very broad category coverage
Multiple monetization and product layers (Pro, Business, Workspace)
Public company with resources to invest in product and growth
weaknesses: High variability in quality across sellers leading to trust issues for some buyers
Relatively high seller commission (around 20%) which can discourage higher-end talent
Commoditization of services making differentiation difficult for sellers
Limited specialized tooling for live gaming/co-play experiences

competitors.name: PlayerAuctions
competitors.description: A long-running player-to-player marketplace for buying and selling game accounts, in-game items/currency, and services like boosting and coaching (including play-with/duo options).

details.funding: Unknown (private; reported investment/ownership links with ItemMania)
details.targetMarket: Gamers worldwide who buy/sell game accounts, in-game currency, items, skins, and pay-for services such as boosting or coaching; sellers range from casual players to professional power-sellers.

items: Listings for accounts, currency, items, skins, and services (boosting/coaching)
Storefronts / seller stores and featured seller promotions
Search and category browsing across hundreds of games
PlayerGuardian escrow system (holds buyer payment until delivery confirmed)
Seller levels/verification and feedback ratings
Dispute resolution and evidence-based claims handling
Account insurance options for some listings
Multiple disbursement methods (bank transfer, crypto options listed), buyer payment gateways
Transaction fees applied to buyers and sellers
Minimum disbursement thresholds and wire fee disclosures
Offer creation and inventory management for sellers
Delivery instructions and in-game delivery workflows
Featured seller advertising slots

missingFeatures: Transparent, published per-product fee schedule (fees vary by product/disbursement and are not always clearly communicated on product pages)
Modern seller APIs and automation for high-volume/pro sellers (bulk listing, inventory sync, programmatic order handling)
Faster/instant payout options and lower minimum withdrawal thresholds — current disbursement minimums and bank fees can be a friction point
Native mobile apps with improved UX compared with the web experience
Stronger long-term guarantees for account sales (many protections are short-term and rely on buyer evidence/disputes)
Advanced analytics/dashboarding for sellers (sales analytics, pricing suggestions, dynamic repricing)

pricingPlans.name: Free
pricingPlans.note: Free to register and create listings — no subscription; Platform earns via per-transaction fees applied to buyers and/or sellers.

pricingPlans.name: Transaction fees
pricingPlans.note: Buyer service fees (to cover fraud protection/escrow/support) and seller fees/disbursement fees apply. Fees vary by product type, payment gateway and payout method (help center lists variable seller fees and disbursement minimums).

strengths: Long-established brand (operating since 1999) with a large registered user base and global reach
PlayerGuardian escrow system reduces buyer fraud and provides an industry-recognized protection model
Supports many game titles and product types (accounts, currency, items, boosting/coaching), consolidating multiple needs in one marketplace
Seller-level system and review/feedback mechanisms help surface more reliable sellers
Operational features like featured seller promotions and multiple payout methods for sellers
weaknesses: Transaction fees, minimum disbursement thresholds, and occasional wire fees frustrate smaller sellers
Protection primarily relies on evidence submission and manual dispute resolution — can be slow and contentious
Selling game accounts and services carries legal/ToS risk with publishers, creating potential platform exposure
Limited modern seller tooling (APIs, bulk-listing automation, analytics) compared to newer competitors
UX and platform experience feel dated in places; mobile/native experience is limited
